hypothesis: consultant:RxBlZhLmOkacNWScmZ6D grants SSH to target
evidence: uid=1000(consultant) on AirTouch-Consultant container

hypothesis: consultant has sudo NOPASSWD:ALL, can reach root on container
evidence: (ALL) NOPASSWD: ALL

hypothesis: flags are on this container
why: this is a Docker container (172.20.1.2/24) — flags likely on the host or other containers

hypothesis: eaphammer certs can be created with --cert-wizard create
evidence: --cert-wizard create requires --ca-cert flag, generated certs manually with openssl into certs/active/

hypothesis: eaphammer evil twin will run non-interactively via nohup
action: nohup ./eaphammer --creds -i wlan0 -e AirTouch-Office -c 44 --auth wpa-eap --negotiate gtc-downgrade --hw-mode a
why: eaphammer reap_creds() calls input() which fails with EOFError in non-interactive context. AP did start successfully (AP-ENABLED) before crashing. Need to patch input() call or use stdin pipe.

hypothesis: eaphammer evil twin on wlan0 ch44 hw-mode a
why: nl80211 driver init failed on wlan0 for 5GHz mode a - wlan0 may not support 5GHz. Need to check phy capabilities per interface.

hypothesis: eaphammer evil twin runs on wlan1 ch44 hw-mode a
evidence: AP-ENABLED on wlan1, hostapd running

hypothesis: deauth frames will force AirTouch-Office clients to reconnect to evil twin
evidence: deauths sent successfully, checking for cred capture now

hypothesis: GTC downgrade evil twin captures cleartext creds from AirTouch-Office clients
why: Both clients (28:6c:07:12:ee:a1, c8:8a:9a:6f:f9:d2) validate CA cert. TLS handshake fails with 'tlsv1 alert unknown ca' before phase 2 negotiation. Need legitimate CA cert or different approach.

hypothesis: container has real CA cert or RADIUS config for AirTouch-Office
action: find / for certs and configs with airtouch/radius/eap references
why: only found system certs and eaphammer test files, no custom CA cert or RADIUS config on this container

hypothesis: AirTouch-Internet has active clients for handshake capture
action: airodump-ng on ch6 targeting AirTouch-Internet BSSID
why: AP visible but 0 clients, 0 data frames. No handshake to capture without active clients.

hypothesis: Docker host 172.20.1.1 has additional services beyond SSH
why: only SSH port 22 open, same OpenSSH 8.2p1

hypothesis: consultant creds work on Docker host SSH via 172.20.1.1
why: connection timed out — SSH on Docker host is firewalled from container network

hypothesis: hostapd has mana/WPE patches
action: searched for hostapd-eaphammer binary and mana strings
evidence: binary is at local/hostapd-eaphammer/hostapd/hostapd-eaphammer but wasn't found by find -executable. Need to check if it exists.

hypothesis: hostapd-eaphammer has mana/WPE patches
evidence: binary references hostapd-mana and hostapd-wpe URLs. WPE patches capture MSCHAPv2 challenge/response during EAP even if client rejects cert.

hypothesis: WPE log captured MSCHAPv2 from cert-rejecting clients
why: Log file never created. WPE captures MSCHAPv2 during PEAP phase 2, but clients reject cert during TLS handshake (phase 1). No tunnel = no inner auth = no hash capture. This approach requires clients to ACCEPT the cert.

hypothesis: PMKID capture on AirTouch-Internet
evidence: hcxdumptool running, waiting for PMKID response from AP

hypothesis: SNMP password RxBlZhLmOkacNWScmZ6D is the PSK for AirTouch-Internet
why: 4-Way Handshake failed - pre-shared key may be incorrect. Associated then disconnected reason=2.

hypothesis: PMKID capture on AirTouch-Internet produced crackable hash
evidence: pcapng file has EAPOL frames from AirTouch-Internet and other APs. Need to convert with hcxpcapngtool to extract PMKID/handshake hashes. Also captured probe from AccessLink and AirTouch-Office BSSIDs.

hypothesis: AirTouch-Internet PSK is in rockyou wordlist
action: aircrack-ng with rockyou-combined.txt on captured handshake
evidence: KEY FOUND! [ challenge ] — PSK for AirTouch-Internet is 'challenge'

hypothesis: Router web panel accepts known creds (admin/consultant with known passwords)
why: all return 200 with 1075 bytes (login form re-rendered). No redirect on any combo.

hypothesis: login.php is vulnerable to SQL injection
action: tested single quote, OR 1=1 with -- and # comments, both single and double quotes
why: all payloads return identical 200:1075 response with 'Invalid Login Details'. Either parameterized queries or non-SQL auth.

hypothesis: Router web app has additional pages/directories beyond login.php
evidence: index.php returns 302 (redirects to login.php), uploads/ returns 403 (exists but forbidden), all other common paths return 404. SSH tunnel from container doesn't work. Need broader wordlist scan.

hypothesis: consultant creds work on SSH to 192.168.3.1
why: Permission denied (publickey,password) — SSH reachable but creds wrong

hypothesis: Broader directory scan reveals hidden PHP pages
action: Python brute force ~300 paths x 6 extensions
why: Only index.php, login.php, and uploads/ found. Very minimal web app.

hypothesis: uploads/ directory contains accessible files
action: brute-forced ~650 file name combos in uploads/
why: all return 404. uploads/ exists but is empty or has unpredictable filenames.

hypothesis: DNS on 192.168.3.1 resolves internal hostnames
action: Python DNS queries for 10 subdomains of airtouch.htb
why: all NXDOMAIN — dnsmasq is likely only serving DHCP/local resolution

hypothesis: AccessLink is a new SSID visible in fresh WiFi scan
action: iw dev wlan3 scan — full 2.4+5GHz scan
why: AccessLink not found in scan. It was likely a client probe request captured by hcxdumptool, not an actual AP.

hypothesis: admin password is common/guessable
action: brute forced 5 usernames x 45 passwords including all known creds, network names, and common router defaults
why: all returned 'Invalid Login Details'. Password is not in common lists.

hypothesis: SNMP has additional useful OIDs beyond initial walk
why: no additional info beyond sysDescr, sysContact, sysName, sysLocation already captured

hypothesis: Login requires session cookie from GET before POST
action: Python session-based login with 9 credential combos
why: all still fail with 'Invalid Login Details' even with proper session cookie

hypothesis: admin password for router login is in rockyou wordlist
action: brute forced 59184 passwords from rockyou with username admin
why: password is not in rockyou. Brute force with common wordlists will not crack this login.

hypothesis: consultant:RxBlZhLmOkacNWScmZ6D authenticates to AirTouch-Office via PEAP-MSCHAPv2
why: TLS tunnel formed (Phase 1 OK), but Phase 2 MSCHAPV2 auth FAILED with consultant creds. Deauth reason=23 IEEE_802_1X_AUTH_FAILED. Need correct enterprise creds, not the SSH password.

hypothesis: Other identity/password combos authenticate to AirTouch-Office PEAP-MSCHAPv2
action: tested 8 combos: admin, admin@AirTouch.htb, consultant@AirTouch.htb, root, airtouch, office, wifi with challenge and SNMP password
why: all Phase 2 Failure. We don't have valid enterprise creds. Need to find them elsewhere.

hypothesis: Router has additional attack surface beyond login.php
action: checked .git, source disclosure, server-status, style.css, LFI, UDP scan
evidence: .htpasswd exists (403), server-status exists (403), style.css reveals post-login UI has menu, file upload, info text. No source disclosure or LFI. UDP has DNS+DHCP only.

hypothesis: eaphammer can relay enterprise auth to capture creds or MITM traffic
evidence: eaphammer has --hostile-portal, --captive-portal, --autocrack, --negotiate modes. No explicit relay/proxy mode. Captive portal could capture HTTP creds. But core EAP cert issue remains for enterprise evil twin.

hypothesis: Hostile portal evil twin captures web creds from AirTouch-Office clients
evidence: Crashed with OSError: /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward is read-only (Docker container). ip_forward already =1. Need to patch eaphammer set_ipforward() to skip the write or use try/except.

hypothesis: Hostile portal evil twin captures web creds from deauthed AirTouch-Office clients
action: eaphammer --hostile-portal with open auth + deauth on 5GHz ch44
evidence: AP started (AP-ENABLED), dnsmasq running with DHCP 10.0.0.100-254, deauths sent successfully on correct channel. BUT: hostapd process died (only dnsmasq alive), Responder failed (missing netifaces). No DHCP leases in dnsmasq log — no clients connected to open evil twin. Clients may refuse to connect to open network when they expect WPA-EAP.

hypothesis: Open hostile portal evil twin captures web creds from deauthed AirTouch-Office clients
why: Enterprise clients won't auto-connect to open network. hostapd died. No DHCP leases, no loot. Open evil twin for WPA-EAP clients is architecturally wrong — they expect EAP handshake.

hypothesis: Container filesystem has hidden creds or certificates
action: searched /home, /root, /opt, /etc — found diagram-net.png and photo_2023-03-01_22-04-52.png in /home/consultant/
evidence: Network diagram reveals 3 VLANs. Corp VLAN 10.10.10.0/24 is behind AirTouch-Office. Router at 192.168.3.1 is 'Tablet manager'. Consultant VLAN is 172.20.1.0/24 (our container). No creds found but critical architecture info.

hypothesis: Corp computer 10.10.10.2 has services with new attack surface
why: all 65535 TCP ports filtered. Host up (ICMP responds). Need to try UDP or access via AirTouch-Office WiFi (Corp VLAN).

hypothesis: Router login uses manager/tablet as username
action: tested 12 username:password combos with manager/tablet usernames
why: all returned same 1075 byte response. Username is not manager or tablet.

hypothesis: Docker host 172.20.1.1:22 now reachable — consultant creds work
why: Permission denied (publickey,password). Port is open (was previously timed out) but consultant creds rejected. No SSH keys found on container.

hypothesis: Router SSH accepts different creds than web panel
why: all Permission denied (publickey,password)

hypothesis: Router has SNMP or RADIUS on UDP
why: SNMP closed, RADIUS closed. Only DNS(53) and DHCP(67) on UDP. No SNMP config leak possible.

hypothesis: Tablets authenticate to HTTP login page — sniffable via ARP spoof
action: ARP scan on 192.168.3.0/24 + launched HTTP sniffer on wlan2
evidence: ARP scan shows only router (192.168.3.1) and us on network. No tablets currently connected. Sniffer running (PID 28197) waiting for traffic. Need to check if tablets connect periodically or if traffic is visible on monitor mode.

hypothesis: Tablets are active on AirTouch-Internet
action: monitor mode sniff on wlan4 ch6 for 12 seconds
evidence: Client 28:6c:07:fe:a3:22 actively exchanging data frames with AirTouch-Internet AP (f0:9f:c2:a3:f1:a7). 14 data frames in 12 seconds. This is a tablet — ARP spoof to intercept its HTTP traffic.

hypothesis: ARP spoofing captures cleartext HTTP creds from tablet
action: scapy ARP spoof gateway for all IPs + HTTP sniffer on wlan2
evidence: Process running but no HTTP traffic captured after 50+ seconds. ARP spoof may not be working correctly — need bidirectional spoofing and IP forwarding for traffic to flow. Alternative: decrypt WiFi frames passively with known PSK.

hypothesis: Passive WiFi decryption with known PSK reveals HTTP creds
action: airodump capture 503 WPA packets + airdecap-ng -p challenge
why: 0 packets decrypted. airdecap-ng needs the target client's 4-way handshake in the capture. Only captured our own station's traffic. Need to capture tablet's EAPOL handshake then decrypt.

hypothesis: Tablet handshake capture enables WPA decryption of HTTP traffic
action: deauth tablet, captured handshake + 60s of data, airdecap-ng decrypted 98 WPA packets
evidence: 15KB decrypted pcap at /tmp/tablet_hs-01-dec.cap. Now need to extract HTTP POST data for login credentials.

hypothesis: Passive WiFi decryption reveals HTTP session — use stolen session cookie to access router
action: airdecap-ng + scapy analysis -> found session cookie, accessed lab.php and index.php
evidence: Session PHPSESSID=j0st31j568ds83kqcph7uh540f with UserRole=user. User 'manager'. Router panel shows WiFi settings (network-name, password, security-mode). All buttons disabled for 'user' role. Logout goes to logout.phtml.

hypothesis: Changing UserRole cookie to admin escalates privileges
evidence: Now shows 'Hello, manager (admin)!' and reveals file upload form: 'Upload Configuration File' with multipart/form-data. IDOR via cookie — client-side auth check only.

hypothesis: File upload gives RCE on router as www-data
evidence: RCE as www-data on AirTouch-AP-PSK. 5 wlan interfaces (192.168.3-7.0/24). No route to 10.10.10.0/24. user account exists. No RADIUS/hostapd configs found.

hypothesis: remote creds give SSH access to AirTouch-Office AP
action: tried SSH from router webshell to 10.10.10.1
evidence: sshpass not installed on router, apt fails (no DNS). Need alternative method to pass password to SSH.

hypothesis: Real CA cert + server key from router enables trusted evil twin
action: extracted certs from /root/certs-backup on router, deployed to eaphammer/certs/active/
evidence: CA: AirTouch CA (ca@AirTouch.htb), Server cert signed by AirTouch CA. Deployed as ca.pem, server.pem, server.key, fullchain.pem. Ready to launch evil twin.

hypothesis: Evil twin with real certs + GTC downgrade captures creds
action: eaphammer --creds --negotiate gtc-downgrade with real CA+server certs
why: Both clients connected and authenticated but refused GTC downgrade — they insist on PEAP (method 25). Need to match their EAP method. Try --negotiate balanced or --negotiate speed to do PEAP-MSCHAPv2 and capture the MSCHAPv2 hash.

hypothesis: Evil twin without GTC downgrade captures MSCHAPv2 via WPE
why: nl80211 driver init failed on wlan1 — stale state from previous AP. Need to reset interface or use different wlan.

hypothesis: Evil twin with real certs captures MSCHAPv2 credentials from AirTouch-Office clients
action: eaphammer --creds on wlan3 with real CA/server certs, deauth both BSSIDs
evidence: Captured MSCHAPv2 for user r4ulcl (domain AirTouch) from client 28:6c:07:12:ee:f3. Hash ready for cracking.

hypothesis: MSCHAPv2 hash for r4ulcl is crackable with rockyou wordlist
evidence: Not cracked with 59K rockyou subset. Need larger wordlist or rules. Trying known passwords and rule-based mutation next.

hypothesis: r4ulcl:laboratory authenticates to AirTouch-Office via PEAP-MSCHAPv2
action: wpa_supplicant on wlan1 with PEAP-MSCHAPv2 creds
evidence: wpa_supplicant started scanning, waiting for association and DHCP. Need to check status.

hypothesis: wlan6 connects to AirTouch-Office
why: NO-CARRIER after 15s. All interfaces support 5GHz. Likely need ctrl_interface in config, or need to wait longer, or EAP is failing silently.

hypothesis: wlan5 connects to AirTouch-Office after monitor cleanup
action: wpa_supplicant on wlan5 after killing all monitor mode
evidence: wpa_state=SCANNING after 15s. No longer EBUSY. Need to wait longer or the AP may have gone down temporarily.

hypothesis: r4ulcl:laboratory on wlan5 with explicit BSSID connects to AirTouch-Office
evidence: stays in SCANNING state after 20s despite AP being visible in scan_results. scan_freq might need different format.

hypothesis: r4ulcl:laboratory connects to AirTouch-Office
why: EAP-Failure reason=23. Identity sent as 'r4ulcl' (6 chars) but MSCHAPv2 capture showed domain 'AirTouch\r4ulcl'. Need to include domain in identity.

hypothesis: AirTouch\r4ulcl:laboratory connects to AirTouch-Office PEAP-MSCHAPv2
action: wpa_supplicant with domain identity on wlan5
evidence: wpa_state=COMPLETED, EAP state=SUCCESS, got DHCP 10.10.10.50/24 on Corp VLAN

hypothesis: SSH to 10.10.10.1 as remote gives shell on AirTouch-AP-MGT
evidence: uid=1000(remote), hostname=AirTouch-AP-MGT. Successfully authenticated. Need to check sudo and find root.txt.

hypothesis: remote user on AirTouch-AP-MGT has sudo or root access
why: remote is not in sudoers. No unusual SUID binaries. admin user exists at /home/admin. Need to pivot to admin or find privesc.

hypothesis: Corp AP has EAP user file or hostapd config with admin creds
evidence: SSH connection dropped - 'No route to host'. WiFi connection on wlan5 may have dropped. Found: hostapd_aps binary running 2 WPE configs from /root/mgt/, EAP user file at /etc/hostapd/hostapd_wpe.eap_user, no capabilities for privesc.

hypothesis: Corp AP EAP user file contains admin creds
evidence: Found admin:xMJpzXt4D9ouMuL3JJsMriF7KZozm7 and r4ulcl:laboratory in EAP user file. Also found hostapd config referencing certs at /root/certs/.
